title: Parallelize unit tests which cannot be parallelized because of NewBuilder()
body:
Certain test cannot be parallized as this uses NewBuilder()
which eventually and indirectly accesses a global variable
defined in `go-selinux`, this must be fixed at `go-selinux`
or builder must enable sometime of locking mechanism i.e if
routine is creating Builder other's must wait for it.

This is a tracker issue for changes in https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/5954

comments.body:
<!-- Answer -->
A practical fix is to add a locking mechanism (like a sync.Mutex) around the section of code in the builder where NewBuilder() interacts with the global variable in go-selinux. This will serialize access and prevent race conditions, allowing tests to run in parallel safely. The lock should be as granular as possible—ideally, only wrapping the minimal critical section that touches the unsafe global state.

Example (pseudocode):

```go
var selinuxGlobalLock sync.Mutex

func NewBuilder(...) *Builder {
    selinuxGlobalLock.Lock()
    defer selinuxGlobalLock.Unlock()
    // existing NewBuilder logic that touches go-selinux global state
}
```

This approach avoids major refactoring and can be implemented quickly. For a long-term solution, consider collaborating with go-selinux maintainers to make the global variable concurrency-safe upstream, which would remove the need for locking in Buildah itself. 

This will unblock parallel test execution and improve CI efficiency as discussed in [PR #5954](https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/5954).
